response:
DESIGN A FUNCTION TO RETURN THE UNIQUE ELEMENTS COMMON IN TWO INTEGER LIST, IN ASCENDING ORDER, WITHOUT USING BUILT-IN PYTHON LIST FEATURES. ENSURE THAT THIS FUNCTION ALSO HANDLES SCENARIOS INVOLVING NEGATIVE NUMBERS. PLEASE WRITE THE ALGORITHM FOR THIS FUNCTION AND MAKE SURE IT IS OPTIMIZED TO AVOID REPEATED ELEMENTS. PROVIDE EXAMPLES DEMONSTRATING FUNCTION USAGE.

*highlighted section*  
THE FUNCTION WILL PROCESS TWO LIST AND FIND THE UNIQUE ELEMENTS COMMON TO BOTH. IT WILL SORT THE RESULT IN ASCENDING ORDER AND HANDLE NEGATIVE NUMBERS WITHOUT USING BUILT-IN LIST FEATURES.  
*highlighted section*  
THE ALGORITHM WILL USE A SET TO STORE ELEMENTS FROM ONE LIST AND THEN CHECK FOR PRESENCE IN THE OTHER LIST. THIS WILL AVOID REPEATED ELEMENTS AND HANDLE NEGATIVE NUMBERS EFFECTIVELY.  
*highlighted section*  
THE FUNCTION WILL ITERATE THROUGH ONE LIST AND CHECK EACH ELEMENT IN THE OTHER LIST. IF THE ELEMENT IS FOUND, IT WILL BE ADDED TO A RESULT LIST. THIS WILL BE SORTED AT THE END TO GET ASCENDING ORDER.  
*highlighted section*  
THE ALGORITHM WILL USE A SET TO STORE ELEMENTS FROM ONE LIST AND THEN CHECK FOR PRESENCE IN THE OTHER LIST. THIS WILL AVOID REPEATED ELEMENTS AND HANDLE NEGATIVE NUMBERS EFFECTIVELY.  
*highlighted section*  
THE FUNCTION WILL RETURN A SORTED LIST OF UNIQUE ELEMENTS COMMON TO BOTH LISTS, HANDLING NEGATIVE NUMBERS AND AVOIDING REPEATED ELEMENTS WITHOUT USING BUILT-IN LIST FEATURES.
